market_overview.headline: Markets showing early reversal signs with SPY and QQQ both in bear rally mode above their 13-week EMAs, while energy dominates leadership amid Iran conflict.
market_overview.detail: 44% of stocks remain in Full Bull signals but major indices are still in bearish EMA structure attempting recovery. Energy minerals showing 100% bullish participation as geopolitical tensions drive sector rotation.

momentum_themes.headline: Electronic Technology Leading Momentum Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology stocks dominate momentum leaders with LITE, CIEN, WDC showing massive EMA spreads above 15 points. Semiconductor and optical networking names benefiting from AI infrastructure demand.

sector_analysis.headline: Clear Energy Leadership as Tech Struggles

strongest.note: Perfect bullish participation as Iran conflict drives oil prices and energy security concerns
Materials benefiting from supply chain diversification and infrastructure spending
Industrial momentum continues with reshoring and defense spending themes

weakest.note: Software and cloud names under severe pressure despite AI hype
Biotech and medical device sectors showing broad weakness
Banking sector mixed as some show reversal signs while others lag

sector_analysis.detail: Clear rotation from high-multiple tech into energy and materials as geopolitical risks favor commodity producers and defense contractors. Traditional growth sectors remain under distribution pressure.

items.text: 237 stocks (79%) still in bearish EMA structures despite recent rally attempts
Technology services showing -64.5% net score with broad-based selling pressure
Major indices still below key EMA levels - this remains a bear market rally until proven otherwise
Iran conflict escalation could trigger broader risk-off sentiment beyond energy beneficiaries

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Markets responded to escalating Iran-US conflict with the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, creating oil supply disruptions and sending energy stocks surging. Geopolitical tensions overshadowed corporate earnings as traders positioned for prolonged Middle East instability. Defense contractors and energy companies led gains while growth stocks remained under pressure.

items.text: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz, creating global oil supply crunch and boosting energy sector
Gulf Coast refining margins surge on increased demand for US fuel exports
Defense spending themes gain traction as NATO allies express concern over conflict escalation
COST shares rise on strong monthly sales amid consumer resilience narrative
AI concerns continue pressuring education sector as students reconsider career paths
